title: Fully-Adhered EPDM System at Roof-to-Wall Transition — 15-Year Performance
success_mode: Fully-adhered 60-mil EPDM membrane with factory-fabricated inside/outside corners and bonding adhesive applied to primed concrete masonry substrate. Membrane carried 8 inches minimum up wall with terminated reglet counter-flashing. Continuous air barrier connection achieved via compatible fluid-applied transition strip at membrane-to-wall junction.
contributing_factors: Proper substrate priming, factory-fabricated corners eliminating field seams at stress points, minimum 8-inch wall carry height, compatible transition materials between EPDM and fluid-applied air barrier, continuous cant strip at base angle
frequency: documented across 6 projects with 10-15 year service records, zero reported leaks at transition
notes: Key differentiator from failed systems is the factory-fabricated corner treatment and the verified compatibility between EPDM bonding adhesive and fluid-applied air barrier transition material. Pull-test values remained above 5 pli at 15-year inspection.
